# SHAFT User Guide

> SHAFT is a unified test automation engine for Web, Mobile, API, CLI, and Database testing.

## Start
- [SHAFT at a glance](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/start/overview): One Java automation engine for Web, Mobile, API, CLI, and Database testing.
- [Install SHAFT](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/start/installation): Generate a new SHAFT project or upgrade an existing automation project.
- [Quick start](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/start/quick-start): Choose the right SHAFT setup path, then run a first test.
- [Upgrade to modular SHAFT](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/start/upgrade): Transactionally migrate legacy or native Maven projects to modular SHAFT.

## Testing
- [Web testing](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/testing/web): A minimal SHAFT browser test with lifecycle, actions, and assertions.
- [How SHAFT reduces flakiness](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/testing/flakiness): How SHAFT reduces common automation flakiness through semantic locators, synchronization, retry evidence, and optional locator recovery.
- [Mobile and Flutter testing](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/testing/mobile): Configure Appium and automate Android, iOS, mobile web, and Flutter applications.
- [UI and API contract replay](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/testing/contracts): Record browser and SHAFT.API traffic, replay captured responses, and validate live traffic against deterministic contracts.
- [API testing](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/testing/api): Build, execute, inspect, and assert REST API requests with SHAFT.
- [CLI testing](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/testing/cli): Execute and validate local, Docker, and SSH commands with SHAFT.
- [Database testing](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/testing/database): Connect, query, and validate databases through SHAFT.

## Agentic
- [Agentic testing](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/agentic/overview): Understand how the IntelliJ plugin, MCP, Pilot, Capture, Doctor, and Heal fit together.
- [Connect shaft-mcp](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/agentic/mcp): Run SHAFT browser, guide search, Capture, Doctor, and healer tools from Codex, Copilot, and other MCP clients.
- [IntelliJ IDEA plugin](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/agentic/intellij): Use the SHAFT coding partner front door for Assistant, Coding Partner, MCP tools, Recorder, Doctor, Healer, Inspector, Projects, and Guide search from IntelliJ IDEA.
- [SHAFT Pilot](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/agentic/pilot): Capture, generation, diagnosis, reviewed repairs, and MCP interoperability — plus downloadable credential-free Pilot example assets.
- [shaft-cli command line](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/agentic/cli): Run all shaft-mcp tools from the command line with one-shot commands or a persistent session.
- [Record tests with Capture](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/agentic/capture): Record browser activity and generate reviewable deterministic TestNG source.
- [Diagnose failures with Doctor](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/agentic/doctor): Build deterministic, portable diagnoses from explicitly allowlisted test evidence.
- [Recover locators with Heal](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/agentic/heal): Enable deterministic, explainable WebDriver locator recovery.
- [Optional AI providers](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/agentic/providers): Consent, redaction, budgets, and provider controls for optional SHAFT AI advice.

## Features
- [Architecture](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/features/architecture): SHAFT runtime, module, facade, and agent integration architecture — plus where SHAFT fits in a general test automation stack and the engine-vs-framework distinction.
- [Features and modules](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/features/modules): Select the core engine and optional SHAFT modules by capability — plus the underlying technology stack and sponsors/adopters.
- [Pillars of successful test automation](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/features/test-automation-pillars): How SHAFT helps teams build scalable, reliable, and maintainable test automation frameworks.
- [Reporting and evidence](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/features/reporting): How SHAFT records actions, screenshots, logs, and Allure results.

## Integrations
- [BrowserStack](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/integrations/browserstack): Choose direct BrowserStack sessions or the optional SDK orchestration module.
- [Visual testing](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/integrations/visual): Add reference-image assertions and image-based touch operations.
- [Desktop and video](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/integrations/desktop-and-video): Optional local desktop recording and SikuliX image-based desktop automation.

## Reference
- [Reference](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/overview): Detailed SHAFT actions, properties, validations, and practices.
- [Alert Actions](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/GUI/Alert_Actions): Handle browser alert dialogs — accept, dismiss, read text, and type into prompt alerts using SHAFT Engine.
- [API Authentication](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/API/API_Authentication): Configure BASIC, DIGEST, OAuth2, API Key, cookie, and session authentication for API tests in SHAFT Engine using setAuthentication and addHeader.
- [Async Element Actions](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/GUI/Async_Element_Actions): Run SHAFT element actions concurrently using Java virtual threads — improve test speed with non-blocking parallel interactions.
- [Basic Configuration for API](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/configuration/apiConfig): Configure SHAFT Engine properties for REST API testing — proxy, timeouts, retry settings, and Swagger/OpenAPI schema validation.
- [Basic Configuration for Mobile GUI](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/configuration/mobileConfig): Configure SHAFT Engine properties for mobile app automation with Appium — Android, iOS, native and web execution settings.
- [Basic Configuration for Web GUI](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/configuration/webConfig): Configure SHAFT Engine properties for web browser automation — browser type, headless mode, timeouts, proxy, and visual reporting.
- [BDD-Style Reports with Allure Annotations](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/guides/BDD_Style_Reports): Use Allure annotations to generate business-readable BDD-style reports without Cucumber — simpler setup, better maintainability.
- [Browser Actions](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/GUI/Browser_Actions): Navigate pages, manage windows, handle cookies, capture screenshots, and control the browser using SHAFT Engine's BrowserActions API.
- [Built-in Cucumber BDD Step Definitions](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/guides/Cucumber_BDD_Steps): Use SHAFT Engine's 500+ pre-built Gherkin step definitions for browser, element, and assertion steps — integrate Cucumber without writing boilerplate glue code.
- [Common Configuration Examples](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/properties/CommonExamples): Practical configuration examples for SHAFT Engine — web, mobile, API, parallel execution, cloud platforms, and CI/CD pipeline setup.
- [Complete Properties Reference](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/properties/PropertiesList): Full reference of all SHAFT Engine configuration properties — web, mobile, API, timeouts, paths, reporting, and integrations.
- [Cross-Platform Strategy: Android and iOS](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/guides/Cross_Platform_Strategy): When to combine Android and iOS tests in one project vs. separate projects, and how to handle platform-specific locators with SHAFT Engine.
- [Custom Properties Generator](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/properties/custom-properties-generator): Generate SHAFT Engine properties files from a searchable catalog of supported properties, defaults, possible values, and descriptions.
- [Database Actions](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/DB/DB_Actions): Execute SQL queries and interact with databases using SHAFT Engine's database automation support.
- [Database Connection Strings](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/DB/Connection_Strings): JDBC connection string examples for MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Oracle, and other databases with SHAFT Engine.
- [Docker Container Terminal](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/CLI/Docker_Terminal): Legacy Docker-wrapped terminal execution using SHAFT Engine's deprecated TerminalActions Docker constructor.
- [Element Actions](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/GUI/Element_Actions): Interact with web elements using SHAFT Engine — click, type, drag and drop, select from dropdowns, handle iframes, and more.
- [Element Identification](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/GUI/Element_Identification): Locate web elements using ID, CSS selectors, XPath, SHAFT Locator Builder, relative locators, shadow DOM, iframes, By objects vs @FindBy, dynamic locators, and cross-platform Android/iOS locators.
- [File Actions](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/CLI/File_Actions): Manage files and directories programmatically — read, write, copy, move, and delete files using SHAFT Engine.
- [Fluent Design: Chaining Actions and Validations](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/guides/Fluent_Design): Learn how to use SHAFT Engine's fluent API to chain actions and validations for readable, maintainable test code.
- [GraphQL API Testing](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/API/GraphQL_Testing): Test GraphQL APIs in SHAFT Engine — send queries, mutations, and subscriptions with variables, fragments, and authentication headers using SHAFT.API.
- [JUnit Integration](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/guides/JUnit_Integration): Run SHAFT Engine tests with JUnit — set up test classes with @BeforeEach, @AfterEach, @Test, and @Order annotations alongside the SHAFT WebDriver.
- [Natural Language Actions](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/GUI/Natural_Language_Actions): Use trust-gated natural-language browser, element, and touch actions through SHAFT Engine.
- [Oracle JDBC Setup](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/DB/Oracle_JDBC_Setup): Step-by-step guide to configure Oracle JDBC driver for database testing with SHAFT Engine.
- [Parallel Execution Configuration](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/configuration/parallelExecution): Run SHAFT tests in parallel using TestNG, JUnit, or Maven Surefire — thread-safe driver management with ThreadLocal and configuration examples.
- [Playwright Backend](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/GUI/Playwright_Backend): Use SHAFT GUI actions and assertions through the Microsoft Playwright Java backend.
- [Programmatic Properties Configuration](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/properties/Programmatic_Config): Configure SHAFT Engine properties in code at runtime — thread-safe programmatic configuration for browser, timeouts, visuals, and behaviour flags.
- [Property Types](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/properties/PropertyTypes): Understand SHAFT Engine property configuration — file-based, code-based, and CLI-based approaches with priority hierarchy.
- [Reporting](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/reporting): Learn about SHAFT Engine's built-in reports — Allure and Execution Summary — how to configure video recording and report behavior via properties, and how to add custom report messages and SHAFT.Report attachments.
- [Request Builder](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/API/Request_Builder): Build and send API requests with SHAFT Engine — GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE with authentication, headers, parameters, body configuration, and request-level retries.
- [Response Getters](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/API/Response_Getters): Extract and parse API response data — body, typed JSON objects, status code, response time, JSON values, and XML values using SHAFT Engine.
- [Response Validations](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/API/Response_Validations): Validate API responses — status codes, JSON values, response body, schema matching, and response time using SHAFT Engine.
- [Running Tests in CI/CD Pipelines](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/guides/CI_CD_Integration): How to run SHAFT Engine tests in CI/CD pipelines — configuring properties, headless execution, and pipeline integration tips.
- [SSH Remote Terminal](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/CLI/SSH_Terminal): Execute commands on remote servers via SSH using SHAFT Engine's TerminalActions with the SSH constructor.
- [Terminal Actions](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/CLI/Terminal_Actions): Execute terminal commands and shell scripts programmatically using SHAFT Engine's CLI terminal actions.
- [Test Artifacts and Report Paths](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/guides/Test_Artifacts): Where to find SHAFT Engine test artifacts — Allure reports, execution summaries, and how to publish them from CI/CD pipelines.
- [Test Automation Solution Design Patterns](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/guides/Solution_Design): Compare test automation design patterns — Page Object Model, fluent design, anonymous classes, inheritance, and base classes — with SHAFT Engine examples.
- [Test Automation Tool Selection Criteria](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/guides/Tool_Selection): Key criteria for selecting a test automation tool or framework, and how SHAFT Engine addresses each one.
- [Test Data Management](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/TestData_Management): Manage test data from JSON, Excel, CSV, and YAML files using SHAFT Engine's test data management API.
- [Test Structure: Cases vs. Scenarios](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/guides/Test_Structure): Understand the difference between isolated test cases and dependent test scenarios, and why you should avoid using priority to order tests.
- [The Testing Pyramid and Automation Strategy](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/guides/Testing_Pyramid): Understand the testing pyramid, the role of each testing level, and how to build an effective test automation strategy with SHAFT Engine.
- [Tips: Infrastructure, Network, and Visual](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/GUI/Infrastructure_Network_And_Visual): SHAFT Engine infrastructure tips — native WebDriver access, custom browser capabilities, mobile emulation, local and Kubernetes Selenium Grid, network mocking, cookies and cached authentication, accessibility testing, Jira integration, and visual testing.
- [Tips: Locators and Self-Healing](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/GUI/Locators_And_Self_Healing): SHAFT Engine locator tips — ARIA role-based locators, self-healing locators (SHAFT Heal and legacy Healenium), Shadow DOM, the SHAFT Locator Builder, Smart Locators, and iframe handling.
- [Tips: Waits and Synchronization](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/GUI/Waits_And_Synchronization): SHAFT Engine synchronization tips — explicit element and browser wait strategies, custom condition waits, and clipboard action sequencing.
- [Touch Actions](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/GUI/Touch_Actions): Automate mobile touch interactions — tap, swipe, pinch to zoom, long press, and app background management using SHAFT Engine.
- [Validations](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/reference/actions/Validations): SHAFT Engine's built-in assertions and verifications — browser, element, file, object, number, and API response validation, JSON schema validation, force fail, and soft vs hard assertion strategy.

## Maintainers
- [Maintainer runbooks](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/maintainers/overview): Build, release, CI, agent guidance, and repository maintenance procedures.
- [Documentation site operations](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/maintainers/site-operations): Develop, validate, and deploy the canonical SHAFT documentation site.
- [MCP publication and deployment](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/maintainers/mcp-deployment): Build, publish, and optionally host the SHAFT MCP server.
- [Agent guidance maintenance](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/maintainers/agent-guidance): Keep repository agent instructions concise, validated, and current.
- [Agent tooling](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/maintainers/agent-tooling): Install, operate, and update the third-party agent stack used for SHAFT maintenance.
- [CI failure investigation](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/maintainers/ci-failure-investigation): Diagnose SHAFT CI failures using workflow logs and Allure artifacts.
- [Maven Central publication](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/maintainers/publication): SHAFT reactor publication order, verification, and rollback contract.
- [Maven reactor](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/maintainers/reactor): SHAFT modules, compatibility artifacts, and build boundaries.
- [Repository history rewrite](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/maintainers/history-rewrite): Maintainer-only procedure for approved repository history rewrites.
- [SHAFT Pilot release](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/maintainers/pilot-release): Release-candidate matrix and publication checks for SHAFT Pilot.
- [Visual provider boundary](https://shafthq.github.io/docs/maintainers/visual-provider): Maintainer contract between shaft-engine and the optional visual provider.
